title: lyonia nipensis subsp. depressinerva
intro: Lyonia nipensis subsp. depressinerva, also known as Swamp Honeysuckle or Southern Honeysuckle, is a shrub native to the southeastern United States. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can be attributed to its rich content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. Consuming the plant is believed to provide various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating digestive issues, and supporting immune function. In herbal preparations, Lyonia nipensis subsp. depressinerva is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to address a range of health concerns.
